content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (like ICM and RND for autonomous agents) can be repurposed to dynamically prioritize what new information gets encoded into an active learning agent’s long-term memory, outperforming 2024 SOTA fixed-marginal or uncertainty-based memory pruning methods. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where agents grow their knowledge base by prioritizing surprising, learnable information rather than just high-uncertainty datapoints.
